SADIQ MAHMUD KHURRAM, J.– Muhammad Rizwan son of Muhammad Munir and Muhammad Usman son of Muhammad Munir (convicts) were tried by the learned Additional Sessions Judge, Haroonabad in the case FIR No. 81 of 2015 dated 23.02.2015 registered at Police Station Faqirwali, District Bahawalnagar in respect of offences under sections 302 and 34 PPC for committing the Qatl-i-Amd of Mst. Shamshad Anjum widow of Muhammad Saleem and Kiran Akbar daughter of Akbar Ali (deceased). The learned trial court vide judgment dated 31.10.2019, convicted Muhammad Rizwan son of Muhammad Munir and Muhammad Usman son of Muhammad Munir (convicts) and sentenced them as under: Murder Reference No. 33 of 2019 Crl. Appeal No. 656 of 2019 2 Muhammad Rizwan son of Muhammad Munir: i) Death under section 302(b) PPC as Tazir for committing Qatl-i-Amd of Mst. Shamshad Anjum widow of Muhammad Saleem (deceased) and directed to pay Rs.200,000/- as compensation under section 544-A, Cr.P.C. to the legal heirs of Mst. Shamshad Anjum widow of Muhammad Saleem (deceased) and in case of default thereof, the convict was further directed to undergo six months of simple imprisonment. The convict was ordered to be hanged by his neck till dead. ii) Imprisonment for life under section 302(b) read with section 34 PPC as Tazir for committing Qatl-i-Amd of Kiran Akbar daughter of Akbar Ali (deceased) and directed to pay Rs.200,000/- as compensation under section 544-A, Cr.P.C. to the legal heirs of Kiran Akbar daughter of Akbar Ali (deceased) and in case of default thereof, the convict was further directed to undergo six months of simple imprisonment.

Feeling aggrieved, Muhammad Rizwan son of Muhammad Munir and Muhammad Usman son of Muhammad Munir (convicts) lodged Criminal Appeal No.656 of 2019 assailing their convictions and sentences. The learned trial court submitted Murder Reference No.33 of 2019 under section 374 Cr.P.C. seeking confirmation or otherwise of the sentences of death awarded to the appellants namely Muhammad Rizwan son of Muhammad Munir and Muhammad Usman son of Muhammad Munir. We intend to dispose of the Criminal Appeal No. 656 of 2019 and Murder Reference No.33 of 2019 through this single judgment. 3. Precisely, the necessary facts of the prosecution case, as narrated by Muhammad Farooq Tahir (PW-1), the complainant of the case, are as under:- “On 23-02-2015 at about 1:00 PM I was present at my home. On hearing hue, cry and noise of firing, I came out from my house. My sister namely Shamshad Anjum was standing in front of the house. Rizwan and Usman accused persons present in the court while armed with 12 bare guns repeater and two unidentified persons while armed with pistols 30 bore were making aerial firing. In my view, accused Muhammad Rizwan made a fire shot with his gun repeater 12 bore which hit on the left flank of sister Shamshad Anjum, who fell down. In the meanwhile, on hearing the noise of firing, Mst Kiran Akbar aged 12/13 years who was coming in the street, tried to enter the home. Muhammad Usman accused made a fire shot with his gun 12 bore repeater which hit to her at between her right shoulder and clavicle bone (collar bono) and she fell down.Two unidentified persons remained making aerial firing which hit the walls of our house. On hearing the hue, cry and noise of firing, Muhammad Saeed s/o Muhammad Younas and Muhammad Rauf s/o Muhammad Amin Caste Arain r/o Chak No.433/6-R alongwith many other people of the same village attracted to the spot and witnessed the occurrence. On seeing the P.Ws accused persons while brandishing their fire arms and making serial firing fled Murder Reference No. 33 of 2019 Crl. Appeal No. 656 of 2019 4 away on their motorcycles. We attended to Mst.

Appeal No. 656 of 2019 4 away on their motorcycles. We attended to Mst. Shamshad Anjum and Kiran Akbar but they succumbed to their injuries at the spot. Motive behind this occurrence was my sister Shamshad Anium had got registered a criminal case F.I.R. No.354/2014 u/s 452/34 PPC at Police Station Faqirwali against the accused persons, due to which above said accused persons committed murder of my sister Shamshad Anjum and Kiran Akbar. I left Muhammad Saeed and Rauf P.Ws to guard the dead bodies and reported the matter at Police Station Faqirwali by submitting a written application Exh. PA signed by me On the basis of my application, F.I.R. was registered, police accompanied me at the place of occurrence and conducted the investigation. After postmortem of the dead bodies of Shamshad Anjum & Kiran Akbar, I.O. Muhammad Azhar Saced SI handed over the dead bodies in lieu of a receipt Exh. PB which is signed by me. ” 4. The learned trial court framed the charge against the accused on 25.05.2015, to which the accused pleaded not guilty and claimed trial. 5. On 09.05.2017 the learned ADPP gave up the prosecution witnesses namely Muhammad Rauf, Muhammad Boota and Muhammad Rafique as being unnecessary. On 11.07.2017 the learned ADPP closed the prosecution evidence after tendering in evidence the report of Punjab Forensic Science Agency, Lahore (Exh.PT) regarding the analysis of the blood stained earth, the report of Punjab Forensic Science Agency, Lahore (Exh.PU) regarding the analysis of the crime empties and the recovered weapons, the copy of F.I.R No.354 of 2014 (Exh.PR) and the report under section 173 of the Code of Criminal Procedure, 1898 of the case F.I.R No. 354 of 2014 (Exh.PS). 8. On 11.07.2017 the learned ADPP closed the prosecution evidence after tendering in evidence the report of Punjab Forensic Science Agency, Lahore (Exh.PT) regarding the analysis of the blood stained earth, the report of Punjab Forensic Science Agency, Lahore (Exh.PU) regarding the analysis of the crime empties and the recovered weapons, the copy of F.I.R No.354 of 2014 (Exh.PR) and the report under section 173 of the Code of Criminal Procedure, 1898 of the case F.I.R No. 354 of 2014 (Exh.PS). 8. After the closure of prosecution evidence, the learned trial court examined the appellants namely Muhammad Rizwan son of Muhammad Munir and Muhammad Usman son of Muhammad Munir under section 342 Cr.P.C. and in answer to the question why this case against you and why the PWs have deposed against you, the appellants namely Muhammad Rizwan son of Muhammad Munir and Muhammad Usman son of Muhammad Munir replied that they had been involved in the case falsely and were innocent. The appellants namely Muhammad Rizwan son of Muhammad Munir and Muhammad Usman son of Muhammad Munir opted not to get themselves examined under section 340 Cr.P.C. however, the appellant namely Muhammad Usman son of Muhammad Munir produced copy of appeal (Exh.DC), copy of suit (Exh.DD), copy of another suit (Exh.DE), copy of application under section 22-A of the Murder Reference No. 33 of 2019 Crl. Appeal No. 656 of 2019 10 Code of Criminal Procedure, 1898 (Exh.DF), copy of order of the same (Exh.DG) and registration book of motorcycle (Mark-D-1) as evidence in his defence. 9. On the conclusion of the trial, the learned Additional Sessions Judge, Haroonabad convicted and sentenced the appellants namely Muhammad Rizwan son of Muhammad Munir and Muhammad Usman son of Muhammad Munir as referred to above. 10. The contention of the learned counsel for the appellants precisely is that the whole case is fabricated and false and the prosecution remained unable to prove the facts in issue and did not produce any unimpeachable, admissible, and relevant evidence. Learned counsel for the appellants further contended that the story of the prosecution mentioned in the statements of the witnesses, on the face of it, is highly improbable. Learned counsel for the appellants further contended that the statements of the prosecution witnesses were not worthy of any reliance. The learned counsel for the appellants also argued that the appellants had been involved in the occurrence due to enmity. The learned counsel for the appellants finally submitted that the prosecution has totally failed to prove the case against the accused beyond the shadow of a doubt. 11.

The learned counsel for the appellants also argued that the appellants had been involved in the occurrence due to enmity. The learned counsel for the appellants finally submitted that the prosecution has totally failed to prove the case against the accused beyond the shadow of a doubt. 11. On the other hand, the learned Deputy Prosecutor General contended that the prosecution has proved its case beyond the shadow of a doubt by producing independent witnesses. The learned Deputy Prosecutor General further argued that both the deceased died as a result of injuries suffered at the hands of the appellants. The learned Deputy Murder Reference No. 33 of 2019 Crl. Appeal No. 656 of 2019 11 Prosecutor General further contended that the medical evidence also corroborated the statements of Muhammad Farooq Tahir (PW-1) and Muhammad Saeed (PW-2). The learned Deputy Prosecutor General contended that there was no occasion for the prosecution witnesses, who were related to the deceased, to substitute the real offenders with the innocent in this case. Lastly, the learned Deputy Prosecutor General prayed for the rejection of the appeal. 12. We have heard the learned counsel for the appellants, the learned Deputy Prosecutor General and with their able assistance perused the record and evidence recorded during the trial., 13. We have noted with grave concern that the ocular account of the occurrence as furnished by the witnesses namely Muhammad Farooq Tahir (PW-1) and Muhammad Saeed (PW-2) is flawed beyond mending and has disfigured the complexion of the whole prosecution case beyond repair and recognition. The statements of both the eye witnesses namely Muhammad Farooq Tahir (PW-1) and Muhammad Saeed (PW-2) are in direct contrast and contradictory to the observations and the opinion of Dr. Ayesha Attique, (PW-11), who conducted the post mortem examinations of the dead bodies of both the deceased namely Shamshad Anjum and Kiran Akbar.

The statements of both the eye witnesses namely Muhammad Farooq Tahir (PW-1) and Muhammad Saeed (PW-2) are in direct contrast and contradictory to the observations and the opinion of Dr. Ayesha Attique, (PW-11), who conducted the post mortem examinations of the dead bodies of both the deceased namely Shamshad Anjum and Kiran Akbar. According to the statements of both the witnesses namely Muhammad Farooq Tahir (PW-1) and Muhammad Saeed (PW-2), they had seen the appellant namely Muhammad Usman son of Muhammad Munir firing once at Kiran Akbar (deceased) during the occurrence and the said fire hit (Kiran Akbar) deceased between her Murder Reference No. 33 of 2019 Crl. Appeal No. 656 of 2019 12 right shoulder and the clavicle bone. Muhammad Farooq Tahir (PW-1) in his examination in-chief stated as under:- “Muhammad Usman accused made a fire shot with his gun 12 bore repeater which hit to her at between her right shoulder and clavicle bone (collar bono) and she fell down.” (emphasis supplied). Similarly, Muhammad Saeed (PW-2) in his examination in-chief stated as under:- “Mst. Kiran Akbar who was coming in the street, on seeing/hearing the firing she was about to enter the home, then accused Muhammad Usman made a fire shot with his gun repeater 12 bore which hit in between left shoulder and clavicle bone (collar bone) of Mst. Kiran Akbar, who fell down.” (emphasis supplied). Contrary to the above referred statements of both the eye witnesses namely Muhammad Farooq Tahir (PW-1) and Muhammad Saeed (PW-2) that they had seen the appellant namely Muhammad Usman son of Muhammad Munir firing once during the occurrence at Kiran Akbar (deceased) and the said fire hit Kiran Akbar (deceased) between her right shoulder and the clavicle bone,Dr. Ayesha Attique, (PW-11), who conducted the post mortem examination of the dead body of the deceased namely Kiran Akbar, observed as many as six independent entry wounds caused by firearm present on the dead body of Kiran Akbar (deceased) and the said entry wounds had different dimensions as well as presence of blackening around them.

Contrary to the above referred statements of both the eye witnesses namely Muhammad Farooq Tahir (PW-1) and Muhammad Saeed (PW-2) that they had seen the appellant namely Muhammad Usman son of Muhammad Munir firing once during the occurrence at Kiran Akbar (deceased) and the said fire hit Kiran Akbar (deceased) between her right shoulder and the clavicle bone,Dr. Ayesha Attique, (PW-11), who conducted the post mortem examination of the dead body of the deceased namely Kiran Akbar, observed as many as six independent entry wounds caused by firearm present on the dead body of Kiran Akbar (deceased) and the said entry wounds had different dimensions as well as presence of blackening around them. Dr. Ayesha Attique (PW-11),on examining the dead body of Kiran Akbar daughter of Akbar Ali (deceased) observed an Murder Reference No. 33 of 2019 Crl. Appeal No. 656 of 2019 13 entry wound of the dimension of 3cm x 3 cm present on the left side of chest, mildly lateral to left and the margins of wound were inverted with blackening and burning present and the sternum was fractured into pieces and also observed another entry wound of the dimension of 2 cm x 1.5 cm present on lateral and front of left chest, just below the left shoulder at the level of left clavicle with the clavicle fractured and the presence of blackening and burning and observed another entry wound of the dimension of 2.5 cm x 1.5 cm present on the front of chest at the level of xiphisternum with blackening and burning and observed another entry wound of the dimension of 1.5 cm x 1.5 cm present on the front of left arm, 8 cm below the left shoulder joint with blackening and burning and observed another entry wound of the dimension of 4 cm x 4 cm present on the lateral surface of left chest with blackening and burning and also observed another entry wound of the dimension of 1.5 cm x 1.5 cm present on the front of left arm, 6 cm above left elbow joint with blackening and burning. Out of these six entry wounds observed by Dr. Ayesha Attique (PW-11),on examining the dead body of Kiran Akbar daughter of Akbar Ali (deceased), the prosecution witnesses namely Muhammad Farooq Tahir (PW-1) and Muhammad Saeed (PW-2) failed to mention as many as five of them. The wounds observed by Dr. Ayesha Attique (PW-11),on examining the dead body of Kiran Akbar daughter of Akbar Ali (deceased), were independent wounds and could not have been the result of a single fire as stated by the eye witnesses namely Muhammad Farooq Tahir (PW-1) and Muhammad Saeed (PW-2).

The wounds observed by Dr. Ayesha Attique (PW-11),on examining the dead body of Kiran Akbar daughter of Akbar Ali (deceased), were independent wounds and could not have been the result of a single fire as stated by the eye witnesses namely Muhammad Farooq Tahir (PW-1) and Muhammad Saeed (PW-2). The deceased namely Kiran Akbar daughter of Akbar Ali received number of Murder Reference No. 33 of 2019 Crl. Appeal No. 656 of 2019 14 firearm entry wounds on front of her body as well as back however the witnesses namely Muhammad Farooq Tahir (PW-1) and Muhammad Saeed (PW-2) spoke of her receiving a single fire. The said opinion of Dr. Ayesha Attique (PW-11) that she had observed as many as six entry wounds on the body of Kiran Akbar daughter of Akbar Ali (deceased) was never challenged by the prosecution either during the investigation of the case or during the course of trial. The prosecution witnesses very well knew that the observations and opinion Dr. Ayesha Attique (PW-11) were in themselves sufficient to shatter the very foundation upon which the prosecution case was built however, even being in knowledge of this fact, no effort was made by the prosecution witnesses to challenge the observations and opinion of Dr. Ayesha Attique (PW-11) or if the same could not be challenged, then to explain the same. Even during the course of investigation the prosecution witnesses namely Muhammad Farooq Tahir (PW-1) and Muhammad Saeed (PW-2) could not explain this contradiction and Muhammad Azhar Saeed Paracha, SI (PW-10), the Investigating Officer of the case, admitted this fatal flaw of the prosecution case during his cross-examination. Muhammad Azhar Saeed Paracha, SI (PW-10), the Investigating Officer of the case, admitted during his cross-examination as under:- “It is correct that I did not note injury in the mid of left clavicle bone and left shoulder of deceased Kiran Akbar however I had noted one injury near to the left shoulder on its left side besides other eight injuries.

Even during the course of investigation the prosecution witnesses namely Muhammad Farooq Tahir (PW-1) and Muhammad Saeed (PW-2) could not explain this contradiction and Muhammad Azhar Saeed Paracha, SI (PW-10), the Investigating Officer of the case, admitted this fatal flaw of the prosecution case during his cross-examination. Muhammad Azhar Saeed Paracha, SI (PW-10), the Investigating Officer of the case, admitted during his cross-examination as under:- “It is correct that I did not note injury in the mid of left clavicle bone and left shoulder of deceased Kiran Akbar however I had noted one injury near to the left shoulder on its left side besides other eight injuries. I had not interrogated the complainant and eye witnesses in this respect as to whether Kiran Akbar having nine injuries on her body and the P.Ws had disclosed only one injury i.e. In the mid of left clavicle bone and left shoulder.” Murder Reference No. 33 of 2019 Crl. Appeal No. 656 of 2019 15 Despite our repeated queries, the learned Deputy Prosecutor General has failed to explain the said discrepancy in the prosecution evidence. In this manner, irreconcilable and harrowing contradictions have cropped up in the ocular account of the occurrence as narrated by the prosecution witnesses namely Muhammad Farooq Tahir (PW-1) and Muhammad Saeed (PW-2) and the medical evidence as furnished by Dr. Ayesha Attique (PW-11). The contradictions in the ocular account of the occurrence, as narrated by Muhammad Farooq Tahir (PW-1) and Muhammad Saeed (PW-2) and the medical evidence as furnished by Dr. Ayesha Attique (PW-11), clearly establish that the prosecution miserably failed to prove the charge against the appellants. The contradictions in the ocular account of the occurrence, as narrated by Muhammad Farooq Tahir (PW-1) and Muhammad Saeed (PW-2) and the medical evidence as furnished by Dr. Ayesha Attique (PW-11), sounded the death knell for the prosecution case and proved to be the cause of its sad demise. Had the witnesses namely Muhammad Farooq Tahir (PW-1) and Muhammad Saeed (PW-2) seen the occurrence then there did not exist any possibility that they would fallen into error. Reliance is placed on the case of “Muhammad Ashraf Vs. Considering all the above circumstances, we entertain serious doubt in our minds regarding the involvement of the appellants namely Muhammad Rizwan son of Muhammad Munir and Muhammad Usman son of Muhammad Munir in the present case. It is a settled principle of law that for giving the benefit of the doubt it is not necessary that there should be so many circumstances rather if only a single circumstance creating reasonable doubt in the mind of a prudent person is available then such benefit is to be extended to an accused not as a matter of concession but as of right. The august Supreme Court of Pakistan in the case of “Muhammad Mansha Vs. The State” (2018 SCMR 772) has enunciated the following principle: “Needless to mention that while giving the benefit of doubt to an accused it is not necessary that there should be many circumstances creating doubt. If there is a circumstance which creates reasonable doubt in a prudent mind about the guilt of the accused, then the accused would be entitled to the benefit of such doubt, not as a matter of grace and concession, but as a matter of right. It is based on the maxim, "it is better that ten guilty persons be acquitted rather than one innocent person be convicted". Reliance in this behalf can be made upon the cases of Tariq Pervez v. The State (1995 SCMR 1345), Ghulam Qadir and 2 others v. The State (2008 SCMR 1221), Muhammad Akramv.The State (2009 SCMR 230) and Muhammad Zamanv.The State (2014 SCMR 749).” Reliance is also placed on the judgment of the august Supreme Court of Pakistan Muhammad Akram Vs. the State (2009 SCMR 230) in which it has been observed in paragraph No.13 of page 236 as infra:- “It is an axiomatic principle of law that in case of doubt, the benefit thereof must accrue in favour of the accused as matter of right and not of grace.
